sections.paragraph:
νοσέω (νόσος) aor. ptc. νοσήσας TestJob 1:2; 35:3 (Aeschyl., Hdt. et al.; SIG 943, 5; pap, TestJob; TestZeb 6:5; ParJer, Tat., Ath.) be sick, ailing in our lit. only fig. (X., Mem. 3, 5, 18 al.; Diod S 11, 86, 3; Heraclit. Sto. 69 p. 89, 20; Wsd 17:8; Philo, Leg. All. 3, 211; Jos., Ant. 16, 244; 18, 25) νοσεῖν περί τι be ailing with, have a morbid craving for someth. (Plut., Mor. 54f ν. περὶ δόξαν) περὶ ζητήσεις καὶ λογομαχίας (s. ζήτησις 1) 1 Ti 6:4.—DELG s.v. νόσος. M-M. TW.

aor. aor. = aorist

ptc. ptc. = participle

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

TestZeb TestZeb = Testament of Zebulon, s. Test12Patr—List 2

ParJer ParJer = Paraleipomena Jeremiou, II? a.d.—List 2

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5
  2. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Heraclit. Sto. Heraclit. Sto. = Heraclitus the Stoic, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

someth. someth. = something

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 678
